# Auto Agent Protocol (AAP) — Full Specification (v1.1)

Canonical URL: https://autoagentprotocol.org
Version: 1.1.0  |  Extension URI: https://autoagentprotocol.org/extensions/aap/v1.1
Transport: A2A `SendMessage` over JSON-RPC 2.0 — sole binding
Discovery: GET https://{dealer-domain}/.well-known/agent-card.json
Built on: A2A v1.0 (Agent2Agent, https://a2a-protocol.org)
License: Specification & schemas Apache-2.0; documentation prose CC-BY-4.0
How to use this file: this is the AAP specification inlined as a single self-contained
document. Each section is meaningful in isolation. The strict machine-readable artifacts
(OpenAPI, MCP manifest, JSON Schemas, agent-card example) are linked at the end and are
AUTHORITATIVE over the illustrative shapes below.

## Table of contents
1. Overview
2. Why AAP
3. Discovery & the Agent Card
4. AAP as an A2A profile
5. Transport & the JSON-RPC envelope
6. Error model
7. Skills
   7.1 dealer.information
   7.2 inventory.facets
   7.3 inventory.search
   7.4 inventory.vehicle
   7.5 lead.submit
8. Consent & lead rules
9. Pricing & FTC compliance
10. Versioning
11. Compatibility (MCP, ADF) & SDK
12. Machine-readable artifacts

## 1. Overview
The Auto Agent Protocol (AAP) lets AI assistants shop at car dealerships. It is a strict
A2A v1.0 (Agent2Agent) profile that defines the typed automotive data shapes AI buyer-agents
and dealer agents exchange when they discover, browse, and submit leads. AAP does not invent
a new wire protocol: a buyer-agent discovers a dealership's A2A agent, calls `SendMessage`,
and every AAP skill defines a strict JSON request and response schema. The payloads travel
inside A2A messages as typed data parts. The whole surface is five skills — dealer.information,
inventory.facets, inventory.search, inventory.vehicle, and lead.submit.

## 2. Why AAP
Buyer-facing AI assistants are becoming a primary way people research and purchase cars, but
dealership inventory and lead intake are locked behind bespoke websites and legacy CRM lead
formats. AAP gives AI agents one open, typed, vendor-neutral contract to:
- discover a dealership and its rooftops (locations),
- read the real, live inventory with the same filters a shopper would use,
- fetch a specific vehicle's disclosed pricing and details, and
- submit a legally consented lead that maps cleanly onto how dealers already take leads
  (including trade-ins and appointment requests).
Because AAP is an A2A profile rather than a proprietary API, any A2A-capable agent can speak
it, and dealers expose it through the same A2A agent card they already publish.

## 3. Discovery & the Agent Card
A dealer agent is discovered exactly the way any A2A agent is: by fetching its Agent Card.

  GET https://{dealer-domain}/.well-known/agent-card.json

There is no second AAP-specific well-known file. The Agent Card is a standard A2A card. A
dealer signals AAP support by declaring the AAP extension in the card's
`capabilities.extensions[]`, using the single canonical extension URI:

  https://autoagentprotocol.org/extensions/aap/v1.1

The card advertises which of the five AAP skills the dealer implements (at least one), the
per-skill request/response schema URLs (in the extension params), and a JSONRPC service
interface in `supportedInterfaces[]` with the endpoint `url`. A copy-pasteable example card
is hosted at https://autoagentprotocol.org/v1.1/examples/agent-card.example.json

No single skill is individually mandatory; the recommended minimum subset for an end-to-end
shopping flow is inventory.search + lead.submit. A small used-car lot might implement only
those two; a franchise dealership might implement all five.

## 4. AAP as an A2A profile
AAP constrains A2A to exactly what automotive retail needs:
- Exactly ONE A2A operation is used: `SendMessage` (a `Message` in, a `Message` out). The
  rest of the A2A surface (streaming, task Get/List/Cancel/Subscribe, push notification
  configs, extended agent card) is out of scope: dealer agents need not implement it and
  buyer agents MUST NOT require it.
- The only transport binding is JSON-RPC 2.0 (`protocolBinding: "JSONRPC"`, REQUIRED). The
  HTTP+JSON (REST) binding that existed earlier was removed in v1.1; gRPC is out of scope.
- AAP payloads travel inside `Message.parts[]` as typed `DataPart`s. Each request and
  response object has a `type` and validates against a published JSON Schema.
An AAP interaction is therefore a normal A2A message exchange whose data parts happen to
carry AAP-typed automotive objects.

## 5. Transport & the JSON-RPC envelope
AAP rides on A2A v1.0, whose single canonical wire format is the ProtoJSON form:
- the JSON-RPC method name is `SendMessage`;
- `role` is the enum name `"ROLE_USER"` (request) or `"ROLE_AGENT"` (response);
- a `Part` has NO `kind` discriminator — it is typed by the member it carries; AAP uses the
  `data` member and sets a `mediaType`;
- every `Message` carries a `messageId`;
- the JSON-RPC `result` is the `SendMessageResponse`, serialized as `{ "message": <Message> }`.

An AAP request object carries its fields at the top level next to `type`. An AAP response
object carries a nested `data` object.

Illustrative request (an inventory.search call):

  {
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "id": "req-1",
    "method": "SendMessage",
    "params": {
      "message": {
        "messageId": "01HZ9F4M7C0X3K5RN8B3WJTW2P",
        "role": "ROLE_USER",
        "parts": [
          {
            "data": {
              "type": "inventory.search.request",
              "filters": { "make": ["Toyota"], "condition": ["used"] },
              "pagination": { "skip": 0, "limit": 20 }
            },
            "mediaType": "application/vnd.autoagent.inventory-search-request+json"
          }
        ]
      },
      "configuration": {
        "acceptedOutputModes": ["application/vnd.autoagent.inventory-search-response+json"]
      }
    }
  }

Illustrative response:

  {
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "id": "req-1",
    "result": {
      "message": {
        "messageId": "01HZ9F4N1JZ7QS8VKR2A3B4C5D",
        "role": "ROLE_AGENT",
        "parts": [
          {
            "data": {
              "type": "inventory.search.response",
              "data": { "total": 1, "skip": 0, "limit": 20, "vehicles": [ /* vehicle objects */ ] }
            },
            "mediaType": "application/vnd.autoagent.inventory-search-response+json"
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  }

The exact field names and required properties for each request/response object are defined by
the JSON Schemas at https://autoagentprotocol.org/v1.1/schemas/ — treat those schemas as
authoritative over the illustrative shapes above.

## 6. Error model
When a skill cannot be fulfilled, the dealer agent returns a JSON-RPC error envelope whose
`error.data` is a typed AAP error object (`type: "aap.error"`) defined by error.schema.json.
Validation errors list EVERY failing field at once in `details.errors[]` (each entry carrying
`instanceLocation`, `keyword`, and `error`) so a caller can fix the whole payload in one pass.

The AAP error code vocabulary (12 codes) and their recommended JSON-RPC codes:
- UNSUPPORTED_SKILL (-32601) — the dealer agent does not implement the requested skill
- SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED (-32602) — the request object failed JSON Schema validation
- MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELD (-32602) — a required field was absent
- INVALID_CONDITION (-32602) — a `condition` value is in the wrong vocabulary
- VEHICLE_NOT_FOUND (-32000) — no vehicle matches the given VIN / stock / vehicle_id
- VEHICLE_UNAVAILABLE (-32000) — the vehicle exists but its status is not available/intransit/pending
- CONTACT_CONSENT_REQUIRED (-32000) — customer info was supplied without required consent
- INVALID_CONSENT (-32000) — supplied consent was malformed, expired, or out of scope
- APPOINTMENT_TIME_UNAVAILABLE (-32000) — the requested appointment slot cannot be honored
- IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT (-32000) — an idempotency key was reused with a different payload
- RATE_LIMITED (-32002) — the caller exceeded the dealer agent's rate limit
- INTERNAL_ERROR (-32603) — an unexpected dealer-side failure
Each error also carries a `retryable` flag; buyer agents MUST honor the value the dealer
returns rather than assuming from the code.

## 7. Skills
Each skill is a single AAP request/response contract carried over the `SendMessage` envelope
in section 5: the request object is a DataPart of the outbound Message; the response object is
a DataPart of the returned Message.

### 7.1 dealer.information (type: dealer.information.request / .response)
Purpose: Return the dealership profile — group name, welcome message, and one or more rooftops
(locations) with their address, geo, contacts, business hours, timezone, and service
capabilities. Anonymous-safe; no consent required. Typical errors: UNSUPPORTED_SKILL,
INTERNAL_ERROR.

### 7.2 inventory.facets (type: inventory.facets.request / .response)
Purpose: Return searchable inventory facets such as makes, models, years, conditions, body
styles, price ranges, mileage ranges, drivetrain, fuel type, and statuses. This lets an agent
learn the dealer's real filterable dimensions before searching. Anonymous-safe. Typical
errors: UNSUPPORTED_SKILL, INTERNAL_ERROR.

### 7.3 inventory.search (type: inventory.search.request / .response)
Purpose: Search vehicle inventory by query, make, model, trim, year, condition, price,
mileage, body style, VIN, stock, features, and availability. The request carries `filters`
plus `pagination` and optional `sort`; the response is a paginated list of matching vehicles.
Anonymous-safe. Typical errors: SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED, INVALID_CONDITION, RATE_LIMITED.

### 7.4 inventory.vehicle (type: inventory.vehicle.request / .response)
Purpose: Return details for a specific vehicle by VIN, stock number, or vehicle_id, including
status, pricing disclosure, photos, mileage, trim, features, fuel economy, and dealer page
URL. Anonymous-safe. Typical errors: VEHICLE_NOT_FOUND, VEHICLE_UNAVAILABLE,
MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELD.

### 7.5 lead.submit (type: lead.submit.request / .response)
Purpose: Submit a consented lead carrying customer info plus any combination of vehicle of
interest, trade-in, and appointment request — a single unified contract that matches how
dealerships actually take leads (e.g. test-drive a new car while getting a trade-in appraised
in the same visit). The request REQUIRES `customer`, a `consent` grant, and a `source_agent`
(the buyer agent's identity). Typical errors: CONTACT_CONSENT_REQUIRED, INVALID_CONSENT,
APPOINTMENT_TIME_UNAVAILABLE, IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT, MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELD,
SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED.

## 8. Consent & lead rules
A lead MUST carry an explicit, valid consent grant for the dealer to contact the customer.
Omitting consent yields CONTACT_CONSENT_REQUIRED; malformed/expired/out-of-scope consent
yields INVALID_CONSENT. Consent is validated on `granted_at`, `allowed_channels`, and `scope`
(scope `["lead_submission"]` is required for lead.submit). The unified lead contract is
deliberate: one submission can bundle a vehicle of interest, a trade-in, and an appointment
request together, mirroring real dealership intake. lead.submit supports an idempotency key so
retries don't create duplicate leads; reuse with a different payload surfaces as
IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT.

## 9. Pricing & FTC compliance
Every Vehicle MAY carry three integer price fields (whole US dollars) so agents never conflate
sticker, advertised, and out-the-door numbers:
- msrp — manufacturer's suggested retail price (informational; set by the OEM)
- list_price — the dealer's advertised base list price BEFORE incentives and fees
- price — the FTC-final out-the-door amount, including all required fees, mandatory add-ons,
  and any conditions on dealer financing. `price` is RECOMMENDED, and it is the only field
  buyer agents use for `inventory.search` `price_min`/`price_max` filters and `sort.field:
  "price"`. Publishing a `price` that omits required fees is exactly the deceptive pattern the
  FTC CARS Rule targets. See https://autoagentprotocol.org/docs/v1.1/pricing-and-ftc.

## 10. Versioning
The current version is v1.1.0, served at /docs/v1.1/* (also aliased at /docs/latest/*). Each
protocol version has its own extension URI; v1.1 is
https://autoagentprotocol.org/extensions/aap/v1.1. Released versions are immutable; frozen
prior versions are v1.0, v0.2, and v0.1 (reachable via the docs version dropdown). Schemas are
versioned under https://autoagentprotocol.org/v1.1/schemas/. See
https://autoagentprotocol.org/docs/v1.1/versioning for the full SemVer policy.

## 11. Compatibility (MCP, ADF) & SDK
- MCP: AAP ships an official MCP reference manifest so the five skills can be exposed as MCP
  tools. See https://autoagentprotocol.org/docs/v1.1/compatibility/mcp and the manifest at
  https://autoagentprotocol.org/v1.1/mcp.json.
- ADF/XML: a submitted lead maps field-by-field onto the legacy Auto-lead Data Format used by
  dealer CRMs, so an AAP lead bridges into existing pipelines without CRM code changes. See
  https://autoagentprotocol.org/docs/v1.1/compatibility/adf-mapping.
- SDK (open source, npm): @autoagentprotocol/types (TypeScript types generated from the
  schemas), @autoagentprotocol/schemas (raw JSON Schema modules), and
  @autoagentprotocol/validator (Ajv-based validators for all AAP objects).

## 12. Machine-readable artifacts
- OpenAPI (JSON-RPC, OpenAPI 3.1): https://autoagentprotocol.org/v1.1/openapi-jsonrpc.yaml
- MCP manifest:                    https://autoagentprotocol.org/v1.1/mcp.json
- JSON Schemas (2020-12):          https://autoagentprotocol.org/v1.1/schemas/<name>.schema.json (e.g. agent-card.schema.json)
- Agent Card example:              https://autoagentprotocol.org/v1.1/examples/agent-card.example.json
- Human docs (v1.1):               https://autoagentprotocol.org/docs/v1.1/intro
- Source repository:               https://github.com/auto-agent-protocol/auto-agent-protocol
